description: Synthetic CONTAMINATED mini-chapter modelled on the prod corpus finding: a grade-9-style geometry chapter 6 whose chunk store also carries a FOREIGN physics chapter (13.x series — 3 figure members) and a SECOND summary block. Deterministic truth: counts include the foreign material (count-as-is + flag, never abstain); contamination fires on (a) foreign major 13 with >=3 members in diagrams and (b) 2 distinct summary blocks. NOTE the deliberately ABSENT case: a same-major merge (two books both numbering 6.x) would NOT fire signal (a) — that is the documented g9 'Lines and Angles' limitation, tested separately.

chunks.chunk_text: 6.1 Basic Terms A line segment has two end points. An angle is formed when two rays originate from the same end point. Fig. 6.1: A line segment PQ and a ray with initial point A.

Fig. 6.2: Intersecting and non-intersecting lines. EXERCISE 6.1
1. In the given figure, lines AB and CD intersect at O. Find the angles.
2. Prove that vertically opposite angles are equal.

Fig. 13.1: The magnetic field lines around a bar magnet. Fig. 13.2: Field lines of the magnetic field through and around a current carrying solenoid. Fig. 13.3: A simple electric motor. Activity 13.1 Take a bar magnet and spread iron filings around it.
SUMMARY In this chapter, you have studied points, lines and angles. Two angles whose sum is 180 degrees are called supplementary angles.
SUMMARY Magnetic field is a quantity that has both direction and magnitude. A current carrying conductor placed in a magnetic field experiences a force.
